agent_output:
```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical cryptographic weaknesses make these API tokens trivially forgeable and vulnerable to timing attacks"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "MD5 used for security-sensitive token generation",
      "risk": "MD5 is cryptographically broken — known collision attacks exist. For a B2B API token that lives 30 days, an attacker with any leaked token-timestamp pair can brute-force the secretKey offline, then forge tokens for any partner.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken and validatePartnerApiToken — crypto.createHash('md5')",
      "reproduction": "Capture one valid token and its timestamp (both travel over the wire or in logs). The search space is `md5(partnerId:timestamp + secretKey)`. With hashcat on a consumer GPU, MD5 hashes at ~60 billion/sec — a 12-char alphanumeric secret is recoverable in hours.",
      "fix": "Replace with HMAC-SHA256: `crypto.createHmac('sha256', secretKey).update(payload).digest('hex')`. HMAC is the correct primitive for keyed message authentication. Alternatively, use a standard like JWT (HS256/RS256) which handles expiry, claims, and signature in one well-audited format.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Secure token generation for B2B partner integrations"
    },
    {
      "title": "Token validation uses timing-vulnerable string comparison",
      "risk": "Direct `===` comparison of token strings leaks information via timing side-channel. An attacker can iteratively guess each byte of a valid token by measuring response times, eventually forging a complete token without knowing the secret.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "validatePartnerApiToken — `return token === expectedToken`",
      "reproduction": "Send repeated validation requests varying one character at a time and measure response latency. A correct prefix byte causes the comparison to proceed one step further, producing a statistically detectable timing difference (~nanoseconds, but exploitable over many samples on a stable network).",
      "fix": "Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(token), Buffer.from(expectedToken))`. Guard the call with a length check first since timingSafeEqual throws on mismatched lengths.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Secure token validation for B2B partner integrations"
    },
    {
      "title": "No token expiration enforcement — timestamp is decorative",
      "risk": "The token embeds a timestamp but `validatePartnerApiToken` never checks whether the timestamp is within the stated 30-day window. Any token, no matter how old, validates forever. A compromised token from a deactivated partner still works.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "validatePartnerApiToken — no expiry check on `timestamp` parameter",
      "reproduction": "Generate a token today. Call `validatePartnerApiToken` a year later with the same timestamp — it returns `true`.",
      "fix": "Add expiry enforcement: `if (Date.now() - timestamp > 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) return false;` before computing the expected token. Better yet, adopt JWT with an `exp` claim so expiry is a first-class concept.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Tokens are long-lived (30 days) — implies they must NOT be valid after 30 days"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "Timestamp not returned from generatePartnerApiToken",
      "description": "The generator uses `Date.now()` internally but never returns the timestamp to the caller. The validator requires the exact timestamp to recompute the token. This means the caller must independently capture `Date.now()` at the same millisecond — a race condition. If the caller captures a different millisecond, validation fails silently.",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken return value"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on partnerId or secretKey",
      "description": "Empty string, undefined-coerced, or colon-containing partnerIds produce ambiguous payloads. `partnerId='a:b'` with timestamp `123` yields the same payload as `partnerId='a'` with a specially crafted suffix. This is a delimiter injection that can cause cross-partner token confusion.",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken — payload construction via template literal"
    },
    {
      "title": "secretKey concatenation instead of proper keying",
      "description": "Appending the secret to the message (`payload + secretKey`) is vulnerable to length-extension attacks with certain hash functions. While MD5 length-extension is secondary to MD5 being broken entirely, switching to SHA-256 without switching to HMAC would inherit this flaw. The fix must use HMAC, not `hash(message + key)`.",
      "location": "Both functions — `.update(payload + secretKey)`"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Adopt a standard token format like JWT (jsonwebtoken library) — it solves hashing, expiry, claims, and validation in one audited package",
    "Store issued tokens server-side with a revocation list so compromised partner tokens can be invalidated before expiry",
    "Add a `kid` (key ID) field so secrets can be rotated without invalidating all live tokens simultaneously"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Any tokens already issued with MD5 are vulnerable from day one. Migration requires re-issuing all partner tokens.",
    "rollback_capability": "Rolling back after switching hash algorithms would invalidate all tokens issued under the new algorithm — needs dual-validation during migration window.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Changing the hash algorithm is a breaking change for all existing partners. Requires a versioned token format or migration period.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging of validation failures — brute-force attempts would be invisible. Add rate limiting and failure alerting on the validation endpoint.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": [
        "Fixing the hash algorithm invalidates all currently issued partner tokens",
        "Adding expiry enforcement may retroactively expire tokens partners believe are still valid"
      ]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy current code. Fix cryptographic primitives, plan a token migration with partners, then deploy with dual-validation (accept old and new format) during a transition window."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "MD5 is unsuitable for security tokens — this is settled cryptographic consensus",
      "Timing-safe comparison is required for secret comparison — well-documented vulnerability class",
      "Missing expiry check is provable by code inspection"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [],
    "assumptions": [
      "Tokens travel over HTTPS (if not, the entire scheme is moot regardless of hash choice)",
      "secretKey has sufficient entropy — if it is a short or predictable string, even HMAC-SHA256 is vulnerable to brute-force"
    ]
  }
}
```
